reviews.titleZh: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities
reviews.summaryZh: EMS 系统、角色与职责，是后面所有内容的职业基础。这一章解释的不是“救护车会做什么”这么简单，而是 EMS 为什么是一个系统、paramedic 在这个系统里扮演什么角色、scope of practice 真正意味着什么，以及为什么 accountability、medical direction、professionalism 和 quality improvement 都和临床工作绑在一起。真正目标是让学生理解：EMS 不是一堆任务，而是医疗系统里的一个受监管专业角色。
reviews.summaryEn: EMS systems, roles, and responsibilities form the professional foundation for everything that follows. This chapter explains how EMS functions as a system rather than as an isolated ambulance response, where the paramedic fits inside that system, what scope of practice really means, and why accountability, medical direction, professionalism, and quality improvement are inseparable from clinical work. The goal is for the student to understand that EMS is not just a set of tasks, but a regulated professional role inside a larger care system.
highlightsZh: EMS 是系统，不是单一车辆反应。
scope of practice 讲的是授权，不是个人自信。
professionalism 和 accountability 属于临床问题，不只是形象问题。
medical direction 把现场 care 和系统监管连在一起。
quality improvement 从 frontline practice 就开始。
highlightsEn: EMS is a system, not a single vehicle response.
Scope of practice is about authorization, not personal confidence.
Professionalism and accountability are clinical issues, not image issues.
Medical direction connects field care to system oversight.
Quality improvement begins with frontline practice.
keyPointsZh: EMS 是一个协同系统，包含 dispatch、response、现场照护、转运、接收机构、监管、documentation 和质量回顾。如果你只理解救护车，你其实还没有真正理解 EMS。
paramedic 或 EMR 的角色，不只是做技术操作，还包括 assessment、priority setting、communication、documentation，以及在明确职业边界内行动。
scope of practice 讲的是你在系统里被训练、被授权、被允许去做什么。它不等于“我觉得我会做什么”，也不由自信单独决定。
medical direction 很重要，因为 EMS care 不是孤立的医学行为。现场照护永远和系统标准、protocol、咨询与监管相连。
professionalism 包括可靠性、边界感、尊重、沟通和有纪律的行为。这些会直接影响病人信任、法律风险、团队协作和安全。
quality improvement 是 EMS 内建的一部分，因为系统会从 documentation、outcome、错误和模式里学习。这意味着每个 provider 都在参与系统改进，即使当下看起来“只是出了一趟车”。
keyPointsEn: EMS works as a coordinated system that includes dispatch, response, on-scene care, transport, receiving facilities, oversight, documentation, and quality review. If you understand only the ambulance, you do not yet understand EMS.
The role of the paramedic or EMR is not only to perform technical interventions, but also to assess, prioritize, communicate, document, and act within defined professional limits.
Scope of practice means what you are trained, authorized, and permitted to do within the system. It is not identical to what you think you can do, and it is not defined by confidence alone.
Medical direction matters because EMS care is not isolated medicine. Field care is linked to system standards, protocols, consultation, and oversight.
Professionalism includes reliability, boundaries, respect, communication, and disciplined behavior. These directly affect patient trust, legal exposure, teamwork, and safety.

Quality improvement is built into EMS because systems learn from documentation, outcomes, errors, and patterns. This means every provider participates in system improvement, even when they are “just doing the call.”
mustKnowZh: EMS 是系统角色，不只是技能包。
scope、accountability 和 medical direction 共同定义安全 practice。
专业行为会影响临床质量。
quality improvement 从普通 call 和普通 documentation 就开始。
mustKnowEn: EMS is a system role, not just a skill set.
Scope, accountability, and medical direction define safe practice.
Professional behavior affects clinical quality.
Quality improvement starts with ordinary calls and ordinary documentation.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：EMS 主要就是 transport。实际上 transport 只是更大 care system 的一部分。
常见误区 2：scope of practice 就是谁觉得自己能做什么。实际上它由系统定义并受监管。
常见误区 3：professionalism 只是形象问题。实际上它会影响信任、安全和 accountability。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: EMS is mainly transport. In reality, transport is only one part of a larger care system.
Common confusion 2: scope of practice means whatever the provider feels capable of doing. In reality, it is system-defined and regulated.
Common confusion 3: professionalism is about appearance only. In reality, it affects trust, safety, and accountability.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic
reviews.summaryZh: paramedic 的 well-being 不是软性附属话题，而是临床可靠性问题。这一章讲压力、疲劳、反复接触死亡、操作风险和长期心理负荷，如何影响 provider 清晰思考、安全工作和长期执业的能力。真正目标是理解：保护自己并不和照顾病人分开，它本身就是持续照护病人的条件之一。
reviews.summaryEn: The well-being of the paramedic is not a soft side topic; it is a clinical reliability topic. This chapter teaches how stress, fatigue, exposure to death, operational risk, and cumulative psychological burden affect the provider’s ability to think clearly, work safely, and continue practicing over time. The goal is to understand that protecting yourself is not separate from caring for patients—it is one of the conditions that makes sustained patient care possible.
highlightsZh: 压力会改变判断和表现。
疲劳属于临床安全问题。
反复接触死亡和创伤会累积影响。
自我保护包含身体、情绪和操作层面。
provider 的生存能力属于系统可靠性的一部分。
highlightsEn: Stress changes judgment and performance.
Fatigue is a clinical safety issue.
Repeated exposure to death and trauma has cumulative effects.
Self-protection includes physical, emotional, and operational domains.
Provider survival is part of system reliability.
keyPointsZh: paramedic 的 well-being 会影响 patient care，因为疲劳、压垮、受伤或 burnout 的 provider，做决策会更差，对风险的承受和处理也会更不安全。
EMS 里的 stress 不只是急性的，它也是累积性的。反复接触创伤、死亡、冲突和操作压力，会随着时间改变一个人。
死亡与濒死在 EMS 里不是偶发例外，而是会反复出现的现实，所以必须有情绪处理与专业 coping。
emergency vehicle operations 和 scene work 不只是任务问题，它们也是个人安全问题。provider 一旦受伤，就无法继续照护别人。
自我保护包括安全操作习惯、尊重自身极限、主动寻求支持，以及及早识别压力已经开始改变自己表现的时刻。
这一章真正教的是：resilience 不是否认压力，而是在反复高压中保持觉察、恢复与可持续工作。
keyPointsEn: Paramedic well-being affects patient care because tired, overwhelmed, injured, or burned-out providers make poorer decisions and tolerate risk less safely.
Stress in EMS is not only acute; it is also cumulative. Repeated exposure to trauma, death, conflict, and operational strain changes people over time.
Death and dying are not unusual exceptions in EMS. They are recurring realities that require emotional processing and professional coping.
Emergency vehicle operations and scene work are personal safety issues as much as task issues. A provider who gets injured cannot continue to care for others.
Self-protection includes using safe operational habits, respecting limits, seeking support, and recognizing when strain is beginning to change performance.
The chapter teaches that resilience is not denial. It is awareness, recovery, and sustainable function under repeated stress.
mustKnowZh: provider 的 well-being 会影响 patient safety。
压力和疲劳同时属于操作与临床问题。
反复创伤暴露会有真实后果。
自我保护是专业责任。
mustKnowEn: Provider well-being affects patient safety.
Stress and fatigue are operational and clinical issues.
Repeated trauma exposure has real consequences.
Self-protection is a professional responsibility.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：well-being 是个人问题，不是专业问题。实际上它会直接影响 patient care。
常见误区 2：burnout 只是累。实际上它可能表现为冷漠、沟通变差、错误增加和安全边际下降。
常见误区 3：自我保护比帮助别人次要。实际上它是可持续帮助别人的一部分。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: well-being is a personal issue, not a professional issue. In reality, it directly affects patient care.
Common confusion 2: burnout only means feeling tired. In reality, it may appear as detachment, poor communication, rising mistakes, and reduced safety margin.
Common confusion 3: self-protection is secondary to helping others. In reality, it is part of helping others sustainably.

reviews.summaryZh: 伤病预防这一章把 EMS 思维从事后反应，拉向预防。它告诉学生：EMS 不只是 response profession，也是一种 public health touchpoint，可以通过教育、风险识别和社区参与来减少未来的 emergency。真正目标是理解：最好的 emergency，很多时候是根本不必发生的 emergency。
reviews.summaryEn: Illness and injury prevention expands EMS thinking beyond reaction and toward prevention. This chapter teaches that EMS is not only a response profession but also a public health touchpoint that can reduce future emergencies through education, risk awareness, and community engagement. The goal is to help students understand that the best emergency is often the one that never has to happen.
highlightsZh: 预防属于 EMS identity 的一部分。
EMS 可以在危机发生前影响风险。
社区教育在系统层面具有临床价值。
public health thinking 属于 EMS。
highlightsEn: Prevention is part of EMS identity.
EMS can influence risk before crisis occurs.
Community education has clinical value at the system level.
Public health thinking belongs in EMS.
keyPointsZh: 预防之所以重要，是因为很多 emergency 的背后都有行为、环境、可及性和教育模式，这些在危机发生前就可以被影响。
EMS provider 常常是在最贴近风险的地方看见问题的人，所以特别有机会识别那些本可以更早处理的模式。
伤病预防不是抽象理念，而是实实在在减少 call、减少痛苦、降低系统负担的方法。
这一章把学生的角色从 responder，扩展成 community health 和 system improvement 的参与者。
预防不是取代 emergency response，而是通过减少可避免 emergency，来增强系统。
keyPointsEn: Prevention matters because many emergencies arise from patterns of behavior, environment, access, and education that can be influenced before crisis occurs.
EMS providers often see risk up close. That makes them uniquely positioned to recognize patterns that could be addressed earlier.
Injury prevention and illness prevention are not abstract ideas; they are practical ways of reducing calls, suffering, and downstream system burden.
This chapter broadens the student’s role from responder to participant in community health and system improvement.
Prevention does not replace emergency response. It strengthens the system by reducing avoidable emergencies over time.
mustKnowZh: 预防属于 EMS 工作的一部分。
public health 和 EMS 有交叠。
在危机前识别模式是有价值的。
系统负担可以在上游被减轻。
mustKnowEn: Prevention is part of EMS work.
Public health and EMS overlap.
Recognizing patterns before crisis has value.
System burden can be reduced upstream.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：预防只属于 public health agency。实际上 EMS 也会贡献。
常见误区 2：EMS 的价值只从 call 开始。实际上预防会在 call 发生前就改变 outcome。
常见误区 3：预防只是额外工作。实际上它会改变系统需求和 patient harm。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: prevention belongs only to public health agencies. In reality, EMS also contributes.
Common confusion 2: EMS value begins only when a call starts. In reality, prevention changes outcomes before calls occur.
Common confusion 3: prevention is “extra” work. In reality, it changes system demand and patient harm.

reviews.summaryZh: 医疗与法律问题这一章告诉学生：临床照护永远发生在法律边界之内，而不是之外。它解释 negligence、accountability、documentation、patient relationship、reporting duty 和法律责任，让学生明白：把事情在医学上做对，和把它做得在法律上站得住、在专业上合适，是分不开的。
reviews.summaryEn: Medical and legal issues teach that clinical care exists inside legal boundaries, not outside them. This chapter explains negligence, accountability, documentation, patient relationships, reporting duties, and legal responsibility so that students understand that doing the right thing medically is inseparable from doing it in a legally defensible and professionally appropriate way.
highlightsZh: 临床行动会带来法律后果。
documentation 同时属于临床和法律。
negligence 看的不是动机，而是有没有达到标准。
patient relationship 会带来 duty。
现场责任可能超出直接 treatment。
highlightsEn: Clinical action has legal consequences.
Documentation is both clinical and legal.
Negligence is about standards, not just intention.
Patient relationships create duties.
Scene responsibilities may extend beyond direct treatment.
keyPointsZh: EMS 中的法律责任，从 provider 接手照护那一刻就开始了，不是等到投诉或诉讼时才突然出现。
negligence 不只是有没有恶意，而是要看在当时情况下，provider 是否达到了应有的 care standard。
documentation 很重要，因为它记录了你发现了什么、做了什么、为什么这么决定，以及病人后来怎么变化。差的记录会同时削弱连续照护和法律防御。
paramedic-patient relationship 会带来真实 duty，包括专业行为、合理照护、沟通和合适交接。
有些场景会带来超出直接 clinical work 的法律义务，比如 reportable case、crime scene 相关责任和公共安全责任。
keyPointsEn: Legal accountability in EMS begins the moment a provider assumes responsibility for care. It is not something that appears only after a complaint or lawsuit.
Negligence is not defined simply by bad intent. It is evaluated by whether the provider met expected standards of care under the circumstances.

Documentation matters because it records what was found, what was done, why decisions were made, and how the patient changed. Poor documentation weakens both continuity of care and legal defensibility.
The paramedic-patient relationship creates real duties, including professional conduct, reasonable care, communication, and appropriate handoff.
Some scenes create legal obligations beyond direct clinical work, such as reportable cases or responsibilities related to crime scenes and public safety.
mustKnowZh: 法律责任开始得很早，不是很晚。
documentation 同时支撑 care 和 defense。
negligence 看的核心是标准。
professional conduct 会带来法律影响。
mustKnowEn: Legal responsibility begins early, not late.
Documentation supports both care and defense.
Negligence is about standards.
Professional conduct has legal implications.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：只要出发点是好的，法律问题就不大。实际上标准和过程同样关键。
常见误区 2：documentation 主要是行政事务。实际上它是核心证据。
常见误区 3：法律 awareness 只在出事后才重要。实际上它从一开始就塑造安全 practice。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: if intentions were good, the legal issue is small. In reality, standards and process matter.
Common confusion 2: documentation is mainly administrative. In reality, it is core evidence.
Common confusion 3: legal awareness matters only after something goes wrong. In reality, it shapes safe practice from the start.

reviews.summaryZh: 伦理问题这一章，训练学生在法律、protocol 和人的现实并不整齐对齐时，应该怎么思考。它讨论 autonomy、professional integrity、resuscitation 选择，以及那些问题不只是“能不能做”，而是“该不该做”的高难度场景。真正目标是给学生一套灰区判断框架。
reviews.summaryEn: Ethical issues teach students how to think when law, protocol, and human reality do not line up neatly. This chapter is about autonomy, professional integrity, resuscitation choices, and difficult value-based situations where the question is not only what can be done, but what should be done. The goal is to give students a framework for judgment in the gray zones of patient care.
highlightsZh: 伦理问的是该不该，不只是能不能。
autonomy 和 beneficence 可能会冲突。
灰区决策需要框架，不是靠冲动。
professional integrity 属于 clinical care 的一部分。
highlightsEn: Ethics is about what should be done, not only what can be done.
Autonomy and beneficence may conflict.
Gray-zone decisions require framework, not impulse.
Professional integrity is part of clinical care.
keyPointsZh: 伦理决策之所以重要，是因为 EMS 里很多场景不只是技术正确的问题，它们还牵涉价值、病人意愿、不确定性和互相拉扯的责任。
patient autonomy 很核心，但不能被孤立理解。capacity、context、风险和专业责任都会改变 autonomy 的解释方式。
停止或不启动某些 intervention，即使法律或流程已有规定，仍可能是很难的伦理问题，因为情绪与专业层面仍然存在。
professional integrity 讲的是即使场景高压、模糊、让人不舒服，你也要维持标准。
这一章真正训练的，不是去找完美答案，而是在不确定中保持清楚推理、能解释自己的决定、并维持伦理纪律。
keyPointsEn: Ethical decision-making matters because many EMS situations involve more than technical correctness. They involve values, patient wishes, uncertainty, and competing responsibilities.
Patient autonomy is central, but it is not interpreted in isolation. Capacity, context, risk, and professional obligation all shape how autonomy is understood.
Withholding or withdrawing interventions may be ethically difficult even when legally or procedurally addressed. The emotional and professional dimensions still matter.
Professional integrity means maintaining standards even when situations are stressful, ambiguous, or morally uncomfortable.
This chapter teaches students not to search for perfect answers, but to reason clearly, justify decisions, and remain ethically disciplined in uncertainty.
mustKnowZh: 伦理和法律会重叠，但不完全一样。
autonomy 必须放进 context 里理解。
灰区需要结构化推理。
professional integrity 在压力下尤其重要。
mustKnowEn: Ethics and law overlap but are not identical.
Autonomy must be understood in context.
Gray zones require structured reasoning.
Professional integrity matters under pressure.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：合法就等于伦理正确。实际上它们会重叠，但并不完全相同。
常见误区 2：伦理太主观，不值得认真学。实际上框架会在难 case 里提升 care。
常见误区 3：patient autonomy 就是病人说什么都照做。实际上 capacity 和 circumstance 都会影响解释。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: legal equals ethical. In reality, they may overlap without being identical.
Common confusion 2: ethics is too subjective to study seriously. In reality, frameworks improve care in hard cases.
Common confusion 3: patient autonomy means doing whatever the patient says. In reality, capacity and circumstance matter.

reviews.summaryZh: 病理生理学这一章，给学生提供伤病背后的生物学逻辑。它不是让你背一堆孤立疾病，而是解释细胞、组织、灌注、炎症、免疫反应、酸碱平衡和应激生理，如何一路连接到 EMS 现场真正看见的 signs。真正目标是让后面的 medical 和 trauma 章节变成“过程”，而不是“清单”。
reviews.summaryEn: Pathophysiology gives students the biological logic behind illness and injury. Rather than memorizing isolated diseases, this chapter explains how cells, tissues, perfusion, inflammation, immune response, acid-base balance, and stress physiology connect to the clinical signs seen in EMS. The goal is to make later medical and trauma chapters understandable as processes instead of lists.
highlightsZh: pathophysiology 解释的是 signs 为什么会发生。
灌注、氧输送和细胞功能深度相连。
炎症和应激反应会塑造很多 emergency。
酸碱问题属于 physiology 问题，不只是化验问题。
highlightsEn: Pathophysiology explains why signs happen.
Perfusion, oxygen delivery, and cellular function are deeply linked.
Inflammation and stress responses shape many emergencies.
Acid-base problems are physiology problems, not just lab problems.
keyPointsZh: pathophysiology 之所以重要，是因为病人的 signs 和 symptoms 不是随机出现的，它们是机体对生理失衡的反应。
细胞损伤、低灌注、炎症、酸碱紊乱和免疫反应，都不是单纯学术内容，它们正是很多 EMS presentation 背后的机制。
灌注失败尤其关键，因为很多 emergency 真正危险的时候，就是组织开始拿不到足够氧和营养的时候。
应激生理和代偿会暂时维持功能，但也会掩盖严重度，直到后面崩掉。
这一章真正训练的是 mechanism-based understanding，让学生后面学各章时，不是死背 disease list，而是能解释它为什么会这样。
keyPointsEn: Pathophysiology matters because signs and symptoms are not random—they are the body’s response to disrupted physiology.
Cell injury, hypoperfusion, inflammation, acid-base disturbance, and immune response are not isolated academic topics; they are the mechanisms behind many EMS presentations.
Perfusion failure is especially important because many emergencies become dangerous when tissues stop receiving enough oxygen and nutrients.
Stress physiology and compensation may temporarily preserve function, but compensation can hide severity until later collapse.
This chapter teaches mechanism-based understanding so that students can interpret the later textbook more intelligently rather than memorizing disease lists.
mustKnowZh: signs 来自 physiology。
灌注失败是很多急症的共同通路。
代偿会掩盖严重度。
机制理解会让后面的章节更容易学。
mustKnowEn: Signs come from physiology.
Perfusion failure is a major common pathway.
Compensation may hide severity.
Mechanism-based understanding makes later chapters easier.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：pathophysiology 只是前临床理论。实际上它直接解释现场 findings。
常见误区 2：重要的是背术语。实际上重要的是理解机制。
常见误区 3：有代偿就说明病人还好。实际上有时只是还没崩而已。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: pathophysiology is only preclinical theory. In reality, it directly explains field findings.
Common confusion 2: the important part is memorizing terms. In reality, the important part is understanding mechanism.
Common confusion 3: compensation means the patient is okay. In reality, compensation may just mean the crash has not happened yet.

reviews.summaryZh: 药理学研究的是药物如何与人体发生作用，以及这些作用如何被安全地用于改善病人结局。在 EMS 里，药理学绝不只是背药名，它真正讲的是：药为什么有效、什么时候可能危险、给药途径和剂量如何影响作用，以及为什么同一种药在不同病人身上会出现不同反应。这一章是后面几乎所有用药决策的基础。
reviews.summaryEn: Pharmacology is the study of how medications interact with the human body and how those interactions can be used safely to improve patient outcomes. In EMS, pharmacology is not just about memorizing drug names. It is about understanding why a medication works, when it may be dangerous, how route and dose affect its action, and why the same medication can produce different effects in different patients. This chapter is foundational because nearly every later medication decision depends on it.
highlightsZh: 药理学的核心是机制和安全，不只是记忆。
药代动力学和药效学解释的是药物作用的不同部分。
剂量、途径、浓度、副作用和相互作用都很重要。
一种药即使适应证对，也可能在错误病人或错误场景里变得危险。
真正的 medication thinking，本质上是 patient-specific physiology thinking。
highlightsEn: Pharmacology is about mechanism and safety, not just memorization.
Pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics explain different parts of drug action.
Dose, route, concentration, side effects, and interactions all matter.
A medication can be indicated and still be dangerous in the wrong patient or the wrong context.
Medication thinking is really patient-specific physiology thinking.
keyPointsZh: 药代动力学讲的是机体如何处理药物：吸收、分布、代谢、排泄。药效学讲的是药物如何作用于机体：受体作用、期望效果和不良效果。真正理解药物行为，这两部分都不能少。
给药途径很重要，因为它决定药物多快、多稳定地到达作用部位。同一种药经静脉、口服、吸入或肌注，表现可能完全不同。
剂量很重要，因为“太少没效果”和“太多出危险”之间，往往就是临床上真正需要把握的界线。很多 medication safety 问题，本质上就是 dose safety 问题。
副作用、禁忌证和药物相互作用绝不是边角信息，它们常常决定了一种本来正确的药，在某个具体病人身上变成不安全的药。
药理学永远和病人本身有关。年龄、体型、慢病、器官功能、脱水状态和合并用药，都会改变药物表现。
好的药理思维不只是问“这个问题该用什么药”，还会问“这药在这个病人身上现在会带来什么风险”。
keyPointsEn: Pharmacokinetics explains what the body does to the drug: absorption, distribution, metabolism, and elimination. Pharmacodynamics explains what the drug does to the body: receptor effects, desired actions, and unwanted effects. You need both to understand real medication behavior.

Route matters because it changes how quickly and how reliably the medication reaches its target. A drug given intravenously behaves differently from the same drug given orally, inhaled, or intramuscularly.

Dose matters because the difference between too little effect and too much effect may be clinically significant or dangerous. Medication safety is often a dose-safety problem as much as a drug-choice problem.
Side effects, contraindications, and interactions are not secondary details. They are often the reason a correct medication becomes an unsafe medication in a specific patient.
Pharmacology is always patient-dependent. Age, body size, chronic disease, organ function, hydration status, and concurrent medications can all change how a drug behaves.
Good pharmacology thinking means asking not just “What drug fits this problem?” but also “What risk does this drug create in this patient right now?”
mustKnowZh: 你必须清楚区分药代动力学和药效学。
药物安全取决于病人，不只取决于药名。
剂量、途径、副作用和相互作用属于核心知识，不是附加知识。
一种药有适应证，并不等于它自动安全。
mustKnowEn: You must clearly distinguish pharmacokinetics from pharmacodynamics.
Medication safety depends on the patient, not just on the drug label.
Dose, route, side effects, and interactions are core knowledge, not extra knowledge.
A drug being indicated does not make it automatically safe.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：药理学就是背清单。实际上它是在理解机制和风险。
常见误区 2：药物和 complaint 对上就一定合适。实际上病人背景可能让同一种药变危险。
常见误区 3：副作用只是小细节。实际上副作用常常决定这药的安全边界。
常见误区 4：用药选择和病人 assessment 是分开的。实际上 assessment 质量会直接决定 medication decision 质量。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: pharmacology is memorizing lists. In reality, it is understanding mechanism and risk.
Common confusion 2: if the medication matches the complaint, it must be appropriate. In reality, patient context may make the same medication dangerous.
Common confusion 3: side effects are minor details. In reality, side effects often define the safety boundary of the drug.
Common confusion 4: medication choice is separate from patient assessment. In reality, medication choice depends on assessment quality.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 8 — Vascular Access and Medication Administration
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 8 — Vascular Access and Medication Administration
reviews.summaryZh: 血管通路与给药管理，是把药理学真正变成临床动作的章节。这一章教你如何建立安全通路，液体和药物如何进入体内，如何准确计算剂量和速率，以及为什么给药是一个 patient-safety process，而不是机械技术动作。它是最偏执行、也最容易一错就出事的章节之一。
reviews.summaryEn: Vascular access and medication administration turn pharmacology into action. This chapter teaches how to establish safe access, how fluids and medications enter the body, how to calculate doses and rates accurately, and how to think about administration as a patient-safety process rather than a technical ritual. It is one of the most operationally important chapters because mistakes here are immediate, concrete, and potentially dangerous.
highlightsZh: 通路只有在安全、通畅、适当时才有价值。
剂量和速率计算属于 patient-safety skill。
给药途径和浓度会改变药物表现。
无菌原则和污染物处理属于治疗安全的一部分。
把药打进病人体内，不如把药正确地打进去更重要。
highlightsEn: Access is useful only when it is safe, functional, and appropriate.
Dose and rate calculation are patient-safety skills.
Route and concentration change how medication behaves.
Asepsis and disposal practices are part of treatment safety.
Getting medication into the patient is never more important than getting it in correctly.
keyPointsZh: 建立血管通路不只是一个操作动作，而是在为液体、药物，甚至必要时的实验室信息建立一条可靠治疗路径。如果通路位置不对、维持不好或适应证判断错误，整个干预都会变得不稳定。
液体和电解质之所以重要，是因为 EMS 常在处理灌注失败、休克、脱水、药物输送和整体生理不稳定，而不是单纯“挂点液”。
给药包含的不只是 route，还包括剂量、浓度、速率、时机、适应证和监测。少考虑一个环节，就会多一层风险。
剂量计算错误属于高风险错误。尤其在儿科、高浓度药物和持续输注药物里，一个小数学错误就可能变成大临床错误。
无菌技术重要，是因为治疗本身也可能制造新的伤害；忽视感染风险、污染或不安全处置，等于把并发风险带进照护过程。
一旦开始给药或补液，就必须继续 reassess。通路可能失效、可能渗漏、药物效果可能与预期不同，而病人状态也可能快速变化。
keyPointsEn: Vascular access is not just a procedure. It is the creation of a reliable treatment path for fluids, medications, and sometimes laboratory information. If the line is poorly placed, poorly maintained, or poorly chosen, the whole intervention becomes unstable.
Fluids and electrolytes matter because EMS is often treating perfusion failure, shock states, dehydration, medication delivery, and physiologic instability—not simply “hanging fluid.”
Medication administration includes route, dose, concentration, rate, timing, indication, and monitoring. Leaving out any of these creates risk.
Dose calculation errors are high-risk errors. Small math mistakes can become large clinical mistakes, especially in pediatrics, concentrated drugs, and infusion-based medications.
Aseptic technique matters because treatment can create new harm if infection risk, contamination, or unsafe disposal is ignored.
A line or medication should always be reassessed after administration begins. Access may fail, infiltration may occur, the drug effect may differ from expected, and the patient may change quickly.
mustKnowZh: 剂量和速率计算是安全关键任务。
给药途径、浓度和适应证必须一起考虑。
建立通路不是终点，后续 reassessment 是硬要求。
只有技术动作、没有安全思维，属于不完整 practice。
mustKnowEn: Dose and rate calculation are safety-critical tasks.
Route, concentration, and indication must always be considered together.
Access establishment is not the end point; reassessment is mandatory.
Procedure skill without safety thinking is incomplete practice.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：通路建立好了，任务就结束了。实际上通路功能和病人反应仍然必须继续监测。
常见误区 2：给药主要是技术问题。实际上它同时是 reasoning、calculation 和 safety 问题。
常见误区 3：液体永远是中性支持。实际上补液在不同生理状态下可能有帮助、可能有害，也可能根本不适合。
常见误区 4：数学错误只是小错误。实际上剂量错误很容易直接变成严重 patient harm。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: once vascular access is obtained, the job is finished. In reality, line function and patient response still need monitoring.
Common confusion 2: medication administration is mainly technical. In reality, it is a reasoning, calculation, and safety process.
Common confusion 3: fluids are neutral support. In reality, fluids can help, harm, or be inappropriate depending on the patient’s physiology.
Common confusion 4: math errors are minor. In reality, dose errors can become major patient harm.

reviews.summaryZh: 人类发展在 EMS 里重要，是因为年龄不只是数字，它会改变生理、沟通方式、心理状态、正常行为、风险模式和 assessment strategy。这一章教学生理解：发展阶段会同时塑造病人的身体和病人接受 care 的方式。真正目标是减少年龄相关误判，并提升对不同年龄层病人的 assessment。
reviews.summaryEn: Human development matters in EMS because age is not just a number—it changes physiology, communication, psychology, normal behavior, risk patterns, and assessment strategy. This chapter teaches students how developmental stage shapes both the patient’s body and the patient’s interaction with care. The goal is to prevent age-related misunderstanding and improve assessment across the lifespan.
highlightsZh: 不同年龄有不同的正常值和正常表现。
发展阶段会影响沟通和合作程度。
儿童不是小成人，老年人也不只是年龄更大的成人。
年龄会同时改变 physiology 和 presentation。
highlightsEn: Different ages have different normals.
Development affects communication and cooperation.
Children are not small adults, and older adults are not just older adults.
Age changes both physiology and presentation.
keyPointsZh: human development 会改变“正常”长什么样，包括生命体征、活动、行为、情绪反应和沟通方式。
不同发展阶段会影响病人如何描述症状、如何面对恐惧、如何忍受不适，以及如何和 provider 互动。
assessment 必须适应病人的年龄现实，而不是把所有病人硬塞进同一个模型。
年龄相关差异不只是心理差异，它们同样是生理差异和安全差异。
这一章为后面的 pediatrics、geriatrics、family interaction 和年龄敏感沟通打基础。
keyPointsEn: Human development changes what “normal” looks like. This includes vital signs, movement, behavior, emotional response, and communication style.
Different developmental stages affect how a patient describes symptoms, responds to fear, tolerates discomfort, and interacts with providers.
Assessment should adapt to the age-related reality of the patient rather than forcing every patient into one model.
Age-related differences are not only psychological—they are also physiologic and safety-relevant.
This chapter builds the foundation for pediatrics, geriatrics, family interaction, and age-sensitive communication.
mustKnowZh: 正常值和正常表现会随着年龄改变。
发展阶段会改变 assessment strategy。
年龄会同时影响沟通和 physiology。
全生命周期思维会提升准确度。
mustKnowEn: Normal changes with age.
Development changes assessment strategy.
Age affects communication and physiology together.
Lifespan thinking improves accuracy.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：年龄主要只改变沟通。实际上它也会改变 physiology。
常见误区 2：一种 assessment style 可以适用于所有年龄。实际上发展阶段很重要。
常见误区 3：年纪大或年纪小只是体型不同。实际上整张临床图都会变。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: age mostly changes communication. In reality, it also changes physiology.
Common confusion 2: one assessment style fits every age. In reality, developmental stage matters.
Common confusion 3: older or younger means just a size difference. In reality, the whole clinical picture changes.

reviews.summaryZh: 病人沟通是临床技能，不是社交附属品。这一章讲的是：沟通质量如何影响病史采集、病人合作、信任、情绪稳定，以及你最终拿到的信息准确度。真正目标是让学生理解：你怎么沟通，会直接改变你学到什么，因此也会改变你做出的决定。
reviews.summaryEn: Patient communication is a clinical skill, not a social accessory. This chapter teaches how communication quality shapes history taking, patient cooperation, trust, emotional regulation, and the accuracy of the information you receive. The goal is to help students understand that how they communicate changes what they learn and therefore changes what they decide.
highlightsZh: 沟通会影响 assessment 质量。
开放式问题常能带来更好数据。
非语言沟通本身带有临床意义。
信任会改变信息质量。
highlightsEn: Communication affects assessment quality.
Open-ended questions often produce better data.
Nonverbal communication carries clinical meaning.
Trust changes information quality.
keyPointsZh: EMS 里的沟通不只是礼貌，而是会改变合作、真实性、清晰度和情绪控制。
provider 怎么问，会强烈影响病人怎么回答，所以好的沟通会直接提升病史质量。
开放式问题通常能比过度狭窄的问题，更好地让病人讲出自己经历的结构。
非语言信号——语气、姿势、表情、沉默、节奏、回避——常会补上语言本身没有给出的信息。
这一章真正教的是：沟通本身就是 patient assessment，也是 patient care。
keyPointsEn: Communication in EMS is not just about politeness; it shapes cooperation, truthfulness, clarity, and emotional control.
How a provider asks questions strongly influences what the patient says back. Good communication therefore improves history quality.
Open-ended questions often reveal the structure of the patient’s experience better than overly narrow questioning.
Nonverbal signals—tone, posture, facial expression, silence, pacing, avoidance—often add information that words alone do not provide.
This chapter teaches that communication itself is part of patient assessment and part of patient care.
mustKnowZh: 沟通属于临床技能。
提问方式会改变答案质量。
信任会提升信息质量。
非语言线索很重要。
mustKnowEn: Communication is clinical.
Question style changes answer quality.
Trust improves information.
Nonverbal cues matter.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：沟通主要看性格。实际上它是可训练的临床技能。
常见误区 2：病人要么知道病史，要么不知道。实际上好的沟通常常能挖出更多信息。
常见误区 3：只听话语就够了。实际上非语言信息也很有分量。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: communication is mainly personality. In reality, it is a trainable clinical skill.
Common confusion 2: the patient either knows the history or does not. In reality, good communication often reveals more.
Common confusion 3: words are enough. In reality, nonverbal information carries weight.

reviews.summaryZh: 研究与质量这一章，让学生看到 EMS 不是只靠习惯和传统运作，而是同时建立在证据、测量、系统学习和安全改进上。它讲 evidence-based thinking、research design、quality indicators 和 patient-safety culture 如何影响真实世界的 care。真正目标是让学习者不只知道 EMS 在做什么，还知道 EMS 怎样判断什么应该做得更好。
reviews.summaryEn: Research and quality show students that EMS is not built only on habit or tradition, but also on evidence, measurement, system learning, and safety improvement. This chapter teaches how evidence-based thinking, research design, quality indicators, and patient-safety culture affect real-world care. The goal is to help the learner understand not only what EMS does, but how EMS decides what should be done better.
highlightsZh: 证据会改变 practice。
quality improvement 属于 care 的一部分，不是和 care 分开的。
patient safety 是系统概念。
研究之所以重要，是因为 practice 应该被检验，而不是被想当然。
highlightsEn: Evidence changes practice.
Quality improvement is part of care, not separate from it.
Patient safety is a systems concept.
Research matters because practice should be examined, not assumed.
keyPointsZh: 研究重要，是因为现场 practice 不能只被习惯、权威或传统引导。只要解读得当，证据会帮助改善 care。
quality improvement 讲的是去看 outcome、模式、错误、延误和系统弱点，好让 care 更可靠。
patient safety 不只是个人错误问题，它也和系统怎么设计、沟通怎么运行、风险反复出现在哪里有关。
即使 EMS provider 自己不做研究设计，也应该理解 evidence 和质量系统为什么重要。
这一章真正训练学生批判性地看待 practice，而不只是执行 practice。
keyPointsEn: Research matters because field practice should not be guided only by habit, authority, or tradition. Evidence helps improve care when interpreted correctly.
Quality improvement means looking at outcomes, patterns, errors, delays, and system weaknesses so that care becomes more reliable.
Patient safety is not only about individual mistakes. It is also about how systems are built, how communication works, and where risk repeatedly appears.
A good EMS provider should understand why evidence and quality systems matter, even if they are not personally designing research studies.
This chapter teaches students to think critically about practice, not just perform it.
mustKnowZh: 证据很重要。
quality improvement 是被落实出来的系统学习。
patient safety 是系统问题。
批判性思维属于日常 EMS。
mustKnowEn: Evidence matters.
Quality improvement is operationalized learning.
Patient safety is a systems issue.
Critical thinking belongs in everyday EMS.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：研究和现场工作是分开的。实际上它会塑造现场工作。
常见误区 2：quality improvement 就是追责。实际上它应该是系统学习。
常见误区 3：patient safety 主要是个人错误。实际上系统会强烈塑造风险。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: research is separate from field work. In reality, it shapes field work.
Common confusion 2: quality improvement is punishment. In reality, it should be system learning.
Common confusion 3: patient safety is mainly about individual errors. In reality, systems strongly shape risk.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 12 — Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 12 — Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada
reviews.summaryZh: 扩展 EMS 系统与社区 paramedicine 告诉学生：EMS 不只是存在于 emergency call 里面，它也可以往上游扩展。这一章解释 EMS 角色如何延伸到预防、follow-up、慢病支持、nontransport 决策和社区健康整合。真正目标是让学生看到：EMS 可以是更大的 health-system participant，而不只是 transport-and-response service。
reviews.summaryEn: Expanded EMS systems and community paramedicine teach that EMS can function upstream of the emergency call, not only inside it. This chapter explains how the EMS role broadens into prevention, follow-up, chronic-care support, nontransport decision-making, and community health integration. The goal is to show students that EMS can be a larger health-system participant, not just a transport-and-response service.
highlightsZh: EMS 角色可以扩展到 emergency response 之外。
community paramedicine 把 EMS 和更长期的健康支持连接起来。
nontransport 需要判断，不是图方便。
系统整合会改变 EMS 能做到什么。
highlightsEn: EMS roles can expand beyond emergency response.
Community paramedicine links EMS with longer-term health support.
Nontransport requires judgment, not convenience.
System integration changes what EMS can accomplish.
keyPointsZh: 扩展 EMS 系统挑战的是这样一种旧想法：EMS 的角色从 call 进入开始，到 hospital transfer 就结束。
community paramedicine 展示了 EMS 如何支持预防、follow-up、医疗可及性和慢病需求，尤其是那些反复进入系统的病人。
nontransport 不是“做得更少”，而是需要更高判断力的决策，前提是 patient safety、follow-up 能力和系统设计都能支持。
这一章训练学生把 EMS 看成一个连续体的一部分，而不是一次性事件。
更大的 lesson 是：EMS 不只在 acute emergency 当中影响 outcome，也可以在之前和之后改变 outcome。
keyPointsEn: Expanded EMS systems challenge the idea that the EMS role starts when the call comes in and ends at hospital transfer.
Community paramedicine shows how EMS may support prevention, follow-up, access, and chronic-care needs, especially for patients who repeatedly interact with the system.
Nontransport is not “doing less.” It is a higher-judgment decision that depends on patient safety, follow-up capacity, and system design.
This chapter teaches students to think of EMS as part of a continuum rather than a one-time event.
The broader lesson is that EMS can help shape health outcomes before and after acute emergencies, not only during them.
mustKnowZh: EMS 可以在 emergency 的上游和下游工作。
community paramedicine 会改变角色 identity。
nontransport 是安全判断，不是偷懒。
系统整合很重要。
mustKnowEn: EMS can work upstream and downstream of emergencies.
Community paramedicine changes role identity.
Nontransport is a safety judgment, not laziness.
System integration matters.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：EMS 主要是 transport。实际上扩展系统会把角色拉得更宽。
常见误区 2：nontransport 代表责任更少。实际上它往往代表判断更多。
常见误区 3：community paramedicine 只是可选附属项目。实际上它会改变 EMS 在 health system 里的位置。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: EMS is mainly transport. In reality, expanded systems broaden the role.
Common confusion 2: nontransport means less responsibility. In reality, it often means more judgment.
Common confusion 3: community paramedicine is an optional side project. In reality, it changes how EMS fits into the health system.

reviews.summaryZh: 气道管理与通气，是生命支持的前门。很多诊断不确定性病人还能短时间撑住，但一个没有通畅气道、没有有效通气的病人撑不了多久。所以这一章真正训练学生的是分层思考：先看 airway 通不通，再看氧气能不能到肺泡和血液，再看病人有没有足够 airflow 来完成氧气与二氧化碳交换，最后再看病人是不是已经开始疲劳、快失去维持这些功能的能力。它真正的教学目标，是在病人彻底崩掉前就把呼吸失败识别出来。
reviews.summaryEn: Airway management and ventilation are the front door of life support. A patient can survive many diagnostic uncertainties for a short time, but cannot survive long without a patent airway and effective ventilation. This chapter therefore teaches students to think in layers: first whether the airway is open, then whether oxygen can reach the alveoli and blood, then whether the patient is moving enough air to exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide, and finally whether the patient is tiring and losing the ability to sustain any of those functions. The real educational goal is to build early recognition of respiratory failure before full collapse occurs.
highlightsZh: 病人即使还在呼吸、还能说话，也可能正在走向呼吸衰竭。
气道、氧合、通气彼此相关，但不是同一个概念。
呼吸工作量、疲劳、意识状态和气道保护能力，往往比单个监测数字更早暴露危险。
吸引、体位、气道辅助器、给氧和辅助通气分别是在解决不同层级的问题。
真正的能力不是会摆设备，而是能及早看出失败并按正确顺序升级支持。
highlightsEn: A patient may still be breathing and talking while moving toward respiratory failure.
Airway, oxygenation, and ventilation are related but not identical concepts.
Work of breathing, fatigue, mental status, and airway protection often reveal danger earlier than a single monitor number.
Suction, positioning, airway adjuncts, oxygen, and assisted ventilation each solve different problems.
The real skill is recognizing failure early and escalating support in the right order.
keyPointsZh: 气道、氧合和通气必须在概念上拆开。airway 讲的是通不通，oxygenation 讲的是氧能不能进血，ventilation 讲的是空气进出和气体交换够不够。把这三者混在一起想，处理就很容易错。
病人还能说话，不代表安全。会说话只说明此刻还有空气在动，不代表 airway 已经稳、通气已经够，也不代表疲劳没有在发展。
气道受损可以来自阻塞、血液、呕吐物、分泌物、水肿、创伤、解剖结构改变或保护反射丧失。意识下降危险，是因为病人可能在完全堵住前，就先失去保护气道的能力。
呼吸工作量是呼吸评估里价值最高的 findings 之一。要看辅助呼吸肌、体位变化、说话断句、浅表呼吸、鼻翼扇动、凹陷呼吸、明显焦虑和疲惫。这些很多时候比 monitor 恶化更早预警。
疲劳是转折点。一个本来很喘、后来突然显得安静一些的病人，不一定在好转，也可能是在把维持通气的肌肉用尽。
吸引是关键 intervention，因为它能把一个被污染、被阻塞的 airway 变成真正能评估、能处理的 airway。很多 airway emergency 里，没先 suction，后面的动作都不会顺。
气道辅助器是支撑设备，不是万能解法。它们能在病人不能稳定维持气道时帮忙撑住，但不能替代持续 reassessment。
给氧和辅助通气的功能不同。给氧可以改善氧合，但如果病人根本没把空气有效进出，还是需要通气支持。
高级气道不是气道管理的起点。基础手法、体位、吸引、辅助器、给氧和 BVM 仍是 foundation。高级选项是当低层支持不够时的升级工具。
这一章最后应该让学生养成一个核心习惯：不要只问“病人有呼吸吗”，而要问“病人现在是不是在有效呼吸，以及五分钟后还能不能继续有效呼吸？”
keyPointsEn: Airway, oxygenation, and ventilation must be separated conceptually. Airway is about patency, oxygenation is about oxygen reaching the blood, and ventilation is about effective air movement and gas exchange. Mixing these together leads to wrong interventions.

A patient may be speaking and still be in danger. Speech only proves that some air is moving now; it does not prove that the airway is secure, that ventilation is adequate, or that fatigue is not developing.

Airway compromise can develop through obstruction, blood, vomit, secretions, edema, trauma, altered anatomy, or loss of protective reflexes. Reduced consciousness is dangerous because the patient may lose airway protection before complete obstruction becomes obvious.

Work of breathing is one of the highest-value findings in respiratory assessment. Look for accessory muscle use, posture changes, inability to complete sentences, shallow effort, nasal flaring, retractions, visible anxiety, and exhaustion. These often warn earlier than monitor deterioration.

Fatigue is a turning point. A patient who looked dramatically distressed and then seems quieter is not necessarily improving; the patient may be exhausting the muscles required to continue ventilation.

Suction is a critical intervention because it turns an obstructed, contaminated airway into one that can actually be assessed and managed. In many airway emergencies, nothing else works properly until suction does.
Airway adjuncts are support devices, not solutions to every problem. They help maintain patency when the patient cannot reliably do so alone, but they do not replace careful reassessment.
Oxygen therapy and assisted ventilation serve different purposes. Oxygen may improve oxygenation, but if the patient is not moving air effectively, ventilation support is still required.

Advanced airway management is not the starting point of airway care. Basic maneuvers, positioning, suction, adjuncts, oxygen, and BVM support remain the foundation. Advanced options are escalation tools for when lower layers are no longer enough.

The chapter should leave the student with one core habit: ask not only “Is the patient breathing?” but “Is the patient breathing effectively, and will they still be breathing effectively five minutes from now?”
mustKnowZh: 每次评估重症病人时，都要把 airway、oxygenation 和 ventilation 分开想。
不要被“还能说话”错误安慰。
疲劳是失败前兆。
给氧不能替代通气。
气道处理是一条升级阶梯，不是设备清单。
mustKnowEn: Separate airway, oxygenation, and ventilation every time you assess a sick patient.
Do not be falsely reassured by speech alone.
Fatigue predicts failure.
Oxygen does not replace ventilation.
Airway care is an escalation ladder, not a device list.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：病人还能说话，就说明气道没事。实际上病人可能已经在疲劳、在肿胀、在出血，或者已经快失去气道保护能力。
常见误区 2：给氧就等于处理了呼吸窘迫。给氧也许能改善氧合，但并不会自动修复通气不足。
常见误区 3：pulse oximeter 看起来还行，病人就安全。实际上病人仍可能正在疲劳、通气变差，或即将快速恶化。
常见误区 4：气道管理就是高级气道。事实上，大多数 airway management 都是从识别、体位、吸引和基础支持开始的。
常见误区 5：辅助通气要等到完全停呼吸才上。实际上等到那时，往往已经错过了更安全的介入窗口。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: If the patient is talking, the airway is fine. In reality, a patient may still be tiring, swelling, bleeding, or losing airway protection.
Common confusion 2: Oxygen fixes respiratory distress. Oxygen may improve oxygenation, but it does not automatically fix poor ventilation.
Common confusion 3: If the pulse oximeter looks okay, the patient is safe. A patient may still be tiring out, ventilating poorly, or heading toward rapid deterioration.
Common confusion 4: Airway management means advanced airway placement. In reality, most airway management begins with recognition, positioning, suction, and basic support.
Common confusion 5: Assisted ventilation should wait until full respiratory arrest. In reality, waiting that long may mean you have already missed the safest intervention window.

reviews.summaryZh: 机械通气，是把气道管理的逻辑进一步延伸到持续呼吸支持。这一章教学生理解：当病人已经不能独立维持有效通气时，支持就必须从短时辅助，升级成有结构的持续管理。这一章本质上不是机器章节，而是讲一台机器如何变成维持生理的工具：降低呼吸工作量、支持气体交换、为 definitive treatment 争取时间，并避免继续恶化。
reviews.summaryEn: Mechanical ventilation extends the logic of airway management into sustained respiratory support. This chapter teaches students that once a patient can no longer maintain effective ventilation independently, support must move from short bursts of assistance into structured ongoing management. The chapter is not fundamentally about a machine. It is about how a machine becomes a tool for preserving physiology: reducing work of breathing, supporting gas exchange, and buying time for definitive treatment while avoiding further deterioration.
highlightsZh: 机械通气是高级呼吸支持，不只是机器操作技巧。
病人的生理状态永远比单独的机器参数更重要。
监测、重复评估和调整是通气管理本身的一部分。
机械通气的存在，是为了在病人无法独立完成有效气体交换时接手支持。
这一章是第13章的延伸，不是跟第13章分开的平行章节。
highlightsEn: Mechanical ventilation is advanced respiratory support, not just a machine skill.
The patient’s physiology always matters more than the machine settings alone.
Monitoring, reassessment, and adjustment are essential parts of ventilation management.
Mechanical ventilation exists to maintain effective gas exchange when the patient can no longer do so independently.
This chapter builds on Chapter 13, not separate from it.
keyPointsZh: 机械通气之所以重要，是因为有些病人已经不能只靠基础支持来维持足够通气。关键问题不是他还有没有呼吸，而是这种呼吸够不够、能不能持续、安不安全。
呼吸机管理本质上是病人管理。参数只有放进病人的 physiology 里才有意义：他是在氧合、在通气、在疲劳、在改善，还是在失败。
机械通气可以减轻已经疲惫的呼吸肌负担，支持肺泡气体交换，并在基础病因被处理前，暂时把病人稳住。
监测仍然是核心。即使病人已经上了 ventilator，provider 还是要反复看胸廓起伏、氧合、通气质量、循环、必要时的意识状态，以及整体趋势。
机器可以支持生命，但如果 provider 只盯数字、不看病人，它也会遮住恶化。临床观察必须永远放在设备信心之前。
这一章真正训练的是一个更大的原则：高级支持不该让 provider 变得更松懈，反而应该让 provider 更警觉，因为现在 stakes 更高。
keyPointsEn: Mechanical ventilation matters because some patients are no longer capable of sustaining effective ventilation with basic support alone. The question is not whether they are still breathing, but whether that breathing is sufficient, sustainable, and safe.
Ventilator management is patient management. Settings only matter in relation to what the patient is doing physiologically—oxygenating, ventilating, tiring, improving, or failing.
Mechanical ventilation can reduce the burden on exhausted respiratory muscles, support alveolar gas exchange, and temporarily stabilize the patient while the underlying cause is treated.
Monitoring remains central. A patient on a ventilator still requires reassessment of chest rise, oxygenation, ventilation quality, circulation, mental status when relevant, and overall trajectory.
A machine can support life, but it can also hide worsening if the provider stops watching the patient and watches only the numbers. Clinical observation must stay in front of device confidence.
This chapter teaches a broader principle: advanced support should not make the provider less attentive. It should make the provider more attentive, because the stakes are now higher.
mustKnowZh: 机械通气是持续性的 physiology support。
要看病人，不只是看机器。
高级支持会增加监测责任，而不是减少。
目标是有效气体交换和降低呼吸工作量，不是把参数调得好看。
mustKnowEn: Mechanical ventilation is sustained physiology support.
Watch the patient, not just the machine.
Advanced support increases monitoring responsibility.
The goal is effective gas exchange and reduced work of breathing, not good-looking settings.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：机械通气只是高级设备操作。实际上它更像是病人生理管理章节。
常见误区 2：参数设对了就说明病人很好。实际上病人可能仍在疲劳、烦躁、低通气或继续恶化。
常见误区 3：一旦上了高级支持，就不用那么频繁重评。实际上恰恰相反，越高级的支持越需要更密切 reassessment。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: mechanical ventilation is just advanced equipment. In reality, it is a patient-physiology chapter.
Common confusion 2: correct settings mean the patient is doing well. In reality, the patient may still be tiring, agitated, hypoventilating, or worsening.
Common confusion 3: once advanced support is started, reassessment matters less. In reality, reassessment matters more.

reviews.summaryZh: 病史采集，是把病人的故事、旁观者背景和周围 scene 里的线索，整理成临床意义的过程。在急救里，history 不只是告诉你发生了什么，它还会告诉你接下来该看哪里、什么最危险、病情是怎么发展来的，以及有哪些背景因素会改变风险。这一章真正训练学生的，是如何主动、高效、有判断地收集信息，让病史变成 clinical tool，而不是形式化 checklist。
reviews.summaryEn: Patient history is the structured recovery of meaning from the patient’s story, from bystander context, and from the surrounding scene. In emergency medicine, the history does not simply tell you what happened; it tells you where to look next, what is most dangerous, how the illness or injury has evolved, and what background factors change risk. This chapter teaches students to gather information actively, efficiently, and intelligently so that the history becomes a clinical tool rather than a formal checklist.
highlightsZh: 好的病史会改变整个 assessment 的方向。
开放式问题通常比僵硬的是非题更容易得到有价值的信息。
病人不是唯一的病史来源。
敏感话题依然重要，只是必须用专业方式处理。
病史本身就是决定后面重点查什么的重要工具。
highlightsEn: A good history changes the direction of assessment.
Open-ended questions often provide more useful information than rigid yes/no questioning.
The patient is not the only source of history.
Sensitive topics still matter and must be handled professionally.
History is one of the main tools for deciding what to examine next.
keyPointsZh: 好的 history 会同时完成三件事：澄清 complaint、暴露严重度，并缩小 provider 下一步最该查的方向。
主诉只是入口。真正的 history 还包括起始、时间、发展过程、伴随 findings、诱发因素、缓解因素、相关既往史、用药背景和 baseline 状态。
在 EMS 里，history 往往来自多个来源。病人也许讲主体故事，但家属、目击者、照护者和环境本身，常常会补上关键空白。
提问方式很重要，因为差的问题只会得到差的数据。封闭式问题有时会太早把方向锁死，而开放式问题更容易让病人讲出真实事件顺序。
敏感话题依然具有临床价值。药物使用、妊娠、自伤意图、虐待和社会背景，都可能相关；因为不舒服就不问，assessment 就会变弱。
这一章最后应该让学生形成一个有纪律的习惯：用 history 去建立时间线、识别危险，并决定 physical exam 现在最该看什么。
keyPointsEn: A good history does three things at once: it clarifies the complaint, reveals severity, and narrows what the provider should examine next.

The chief complaint is only a doorway. The real history includes onset, timing, progression, associated findings, precipitating factors, relieving factors, relevant past history, medication context, and baseline state.
In EMS, history often comes from multiple sources. The patient may provide the central story, but family, witnesses, caregivers, and the environment may each add crucial missing information.
Questioning style matters because poor questions produce poor data. Closed questions may narrow things too early, while open-ended questions often reveal the patient’s true sequence of events.

Sensitive topics remain clinically important. Drug use, pregnancy, self-harm intent, abuse, and social context may all be relevant, and avoiding them because they are uncomfortable weakens the assessment.
The chapter should leave students with a disciplined habit: use the history to build a timeline, identify danger, and decide what part of the physical exam now matters most.
mustKnowZh: history 应该塑造 exam。
多个信息来源都重要。
complaint 不等于完整 history。
提问方式会改变信息质量。
mustKnowEn: History should shape the exam.
Multiple information sources matter.
The complaint is not the whole history.
Question style changes information quality.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：病史采集就是照着模板问。实际上它是一个需要灵活调整的临床技能。
常见误区 2：查体总比病史更重要。实际上差的病史会把整个查体带偏。
常见误区 3：敏感话题最好别问。实际上重要话题还是得问，只是要专业、合适地问。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: history taking is a script. In reality, it is a flexible clinical skill.
Common confusion 2: the physical exam is always more important than the history. In reality, a poor history can misdirect the entire exam.
Common confusion 3: sensitive topics should be avoided. In reality, important topics still need to be addressed—professionally and appropriately.

reviews.summaryZh: 体格检查，是把直接观察转化成临床意义的过程。它不是一串动作的堆积，也不是在病人身上摸了几下就算完成。这一章真正训练学生的是：收集相关 findings、把它们放进 context 里解释，并让 exam 去回答临床问题，而不是完成仪式。只有当 exam 真正改变理解、改变优先级或支持决策时，它才有价值。
reviews.summaryEn: Physical examination is the conversion of direct observation into clinical meaning. It is not simply a series of maneuvers, and it is not finished when the provider has touched the patient in several places. This chapter teaches students to collect relevant findings, interpret them in context, and use the exam to answer clinical questions rather than to perform rituals. The exam becomes useful only when it changes understanding, changes priority, or confirms a decision.
highlightsZh: 整体印象往往是最早也最重要的 findings 之一。
生命体征的价值在趋势，不在单个数字。
体格检查必须和病史、现场一起解读。
做得很全，不等于做得很有用。
查体本身有局限，所以必须放在 context 里看。
highlightsEn: General impression is one of the earliest and most important findings.
Vital signs matter as trends, not isolated numbers.
The physical exam must be connected to the history and scene.
A complete exam is not the same as a useful exam.
The exam has limits and must always be interpreted in context.
keyPointsZh: 整体印象往往是第一个真正有意义的 physical finding。很多时候还没开始 full exam，病人的外观就已经在告诉你：这是小问题、重问题，还是马上危险的问题。
生命体征应该被理解成放进 context 里的生理信号，而不是孤立数字。它们的 pattern、trend，以及和病人其他表现之间的关系，比单个数值本身更重要。
focused physical exam 的目的，是先回答最紧急的问题。只有当病情和 scene 允许时，detailed exam 才真正有价值。
体格检查有局限。看起来正常的 exam，不一定等于低风险病人，尤其是在正在演变的疾病或隐藏创伤里。
exam 永远都应该和 history 合在一起解释。没有 context 的 looking，是很弱的 medicine；context 会把 findings 变成有意义的信息。
学生学完这一章后应该明白：exam 不是“每个部位都看过了”就算 complete，而是“它回答了此刻最重要的 clinical question”才算 complete。
keyPointsEn: General impression is often the first meaningful physical finding. Before a full exam begins, the patient’s appearance may already tell you whether the problem is minor, serious, or immediately dangerous.
Vital signs should be understood as physiologic signals in context, not as isolated numbers. Their pattern, trend, and relationship to the rest of the patient matter more than one value by itself.
A focused physical exam is designed to answer the most urgent question first. A detailed exam is useful only when the patient’s condition and scene allow it.
Physical examination has limits. A normal-looking exam does not always mean a low-risk patient, especially early in evolving disease or hidden trauma.
The exam should always be integrated with history. Looking without context is weak medicine; context turns findings into meaning.

Students should leave this chapter knowing that an exam is not “complete” when every body part was checked—it is complete when the exam answered the important clinical questions that mattered in that moment.
mustKnowZh: 整体印象属于真实 exam data。
生命体征需要 context 和 trend。
focused exam 优先于 exhaustive exam。
exam 要回答问题，不是完成仪式。
mustKnowEn: General impression is real exam data.
Vital signs require context and trend.
Focused exam comes before exhaustive exam.
The exam must answer a question, not complete a ritual.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：查得越多就越好。实际上相关性比数量更重要。
常见误区 2：一个生命体征正常就说明安全。实际上 context 和趋势更重要。
常见误区 3：体格检查可以单独成立。实际上它必须和病史、现场和 reassessment 一起用。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: more exam always means better exam. In reality, relevance matters more than volume.
Common confusion 2: a single normal vital sign means safety. In reality, context and trend matter more.
Common confusion 3: physical examination can stand alone. In reality, it must be integrated with history, scene, and reassessment.

reviews.summaryZh: 病人评估，是让整本书其他章节真正变得可临床使用的框架。它把 scene awareness、initial assessment、focused exam、detailed exam 和 ongoing reassessment 串成一个可重复的方法，用来理解那些每分钟都可能在变化的病人。这一章不是让你背一条静止顺序，而是训练你在病人不稳、scene 很难、clinical picture 还不清楚时，如何重新排序 assessment。
reviews.summaryEn: Patient assessment is the framework that makes all other chapters clinically usable. It links scene awareness, initial assessment, focused examination, detailed examination, and ongoing reassessment into one repeatable method for understanding a patient whose condition may change minute to minute. This chapter is not about memorizing a static sequence, but about learning how to prioritize and restructure your assessment when the patient is unstable, the scene is difficult, or the clinical picture is still unclear.
highlightsZh: assessment 有结构，但也是动态的。
病人还没开口，scene 就已经在给你信息。
initial assessment 决定优先级和紧迫度。
focused assessment 和 detailed assessment 负责不同任务。
reassessment 是病人“开始暴露自己变化”的地方。
highlightsEn: Assessment is structured, but it is also dynamic.
The scene gives you information before the patient even speaks.
Initial assessment sets priority and urgency.
Focused assessment and detailed assessment have different jobs.
Reassessment is where changing patients declare themselves.
keyPointsZh: assessment 在真正接触病人前就开始了。scene、机制、环境、旁观者和第一眼看到的线索，都已经在建立 clinical picture。
initial assessment 是第一轮分拣：谁稳定，谁不稳，什么东西现在会先杀人，什么不能等到 detailed exam 再说。
focused assessment 之所以要有选择性，是因为急救依赖于先回答最危险的问题，而不是最有趣的问题。
detailed assessment 只有在优先级处理后，才是增加深度的价值。它当然重要，但不能抢走 urgent threat 的时间。
reassessment 不是重复 paperwork，而是承认病人是动态的，会随着时间暴露更多问题，常常通过恶化、疲劳或 pattern 改变来表现。
这一章真正训练学生分层思考：我现在知道什么？现在最重要的是什么？正在变化什么？还有什么关键信息没拿到？
keyPointsEn: Assessment begins before touch. The scene, mechanism, environment, bystanders, and initial visual cues already start building a clinical picture.
The initial assessment is the first sorting step: who is stable, who is unstable, what threatens life now, and what cannot wait for a detailed exam.
Focused assessment is selective because urgent care depends on answering the most dangerous question first, not the most interesting one.
Detailed assessment adds depth only after priorities are managed. It is valuable, but it should never steal time from urgent threats.
Reassessment is not repetitive paperwork. It is the recognition that dynamic patients reveal themselves over time, often by worsening, tiring, or changing pattern.
The chapter teaches students to think in layers: what do I know now, what matters now, what is changing, and what information is still missing?
mustKnowZh: assessment 是动态的，不是僵硬的。
优先级高于完整度。
scene information 就是 clinical information。
不稳定病人的真相，很多时候是靠 reassessment 暴露出来的。
mustKnowEn: Assessment is dynamic, not rigid.
Priorities outrank completeness.
Scene information is clinical information.
Reassessment is how unstable patients reveal the truth.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：每个病人都必须照完全相同顺序评估。实际上优先级会随着病情严重度变化。
常见误区 2：reassessment 只是为了文书。实际上它是识别恶化最主要的方式之一。
常见误区 3：scene assessment 只是安全检查。实际上它也会告诉你机制、紧迫度和可能的病理方向。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: every patient should be assessed in the same exact order. In reality, priorities change with acuity.
Common confusion 2: reassessment is only for documentation. In reality, it is one of the main ways worsening is recognized.
Common confusion 3: scene assessment is just about hazards. In reality, it also informs mechanism, urgency, and likely pathology.

reviews.summaryZh: 临床决策，是把信息转换成行动的过程。这一章教学生明白：最好的决定，不一定是诊断最漂亮的决定，而是当下最能保护病人的那个决定。学生要学会把 signs、symptoms、history、mechanism、context 和 protocols 整合起来，在不确定中做对优先级。真正的 lesson 是：EMS 里的 decision-making，很多时候是在选最安全的下一步，而不是立刻把整张拼图一次拼完。
reviews.summaryEn: Clinical decisions transform information into action. This chapter teaches that the best decision is not always the one with the most elegant diagnosis, but the one that best protects the patient in the moment. Students learn how to integrate signs, symptoms, history, mechanism, context, and protocols in order to prioritize correctly under uncertainty. The real lesson is that decision-making in EMS is often about choosing the safest next move, not about finishing the entire puzzle immediately.
highlightsZh: 优先级往往比精确命名更早重要。
最可能诊断不一定等于最重要下一步。
protocol 引导决策，但不能代替判断。
临床推理核心在识别危险，而不只是贴标签。
把体征、病史和 context 合起来，决策才会更稳。
highlightsEn: Priority often matters before precision.
The most likely diagnosis is not always the most important next move.
Protocols guide decisions but do not replace judgment.
Clinical reasoning is about danger recognition, not just label assignment.
Decision-making improves when signs, history, and context are integrated.
keyPointsZh: 临床决策建立在优先级排序上。provider 先要问的，是现在什么危险、什么在变、什么不能等。
一个 diagnosis 还没完全清楚时，下一步最好的动作却可能已经很清楚。EMS 常常要求在不完全确定前就行动。
protocol 提供结构和安全边界，但并不会取消判断。好的 protocol 使用是有思考的，不是机器执行。
当 provider 把 history、exam、scene、trend 和 physiology 整合起来，而不是只抓一个点时，decision quality 会明显变好。
这一章真正训练学生超越 labels 去思考：不只问“这是什么”，还问“威胁是什么”“方向是什么”“下一步该发生什么”。
这也是把 textbook knowledge 开始变成 field judgment 的章节。
keyPointsEn: Clinical decisions are built on prioritization. The provider must first ask what is dangerous, what is changing, and what cannot wait.
A diagnosis may still be incomplete while the next best action is already clear. EMS often requires action before full certainty exists.
Protocols create structure and safety boundaries, but they do not remove the need for judgment. Good protocol use is thoughtful, not robotic.
Decision quality improves when the provider integrates history, exam, scene, trend, and physiology rather than relying on one piece of information.
The chapter teaches the student to think beyond labels: not just “What is this?” but “What is the threat? What is the direction? What should happen next?”
This is the chapter that begins turning textbook knowledge into field judgment.
mustKnowZh: 最安全的下一步，常常比最精确的标签更重要。
protocol 支持判断，但不能替代判断。
优先级排序是 field decision-making 的核心。
临床决策必须在不确定中完成。
mustKnowEn: The safest next step often matters more than the most precise label.
Protocols support judgment; they do not replace it.
Prioritization is the heart of field decision-making.
Clinical decisions must be made under uncertainty.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：诊断总是第一目标。实际上第一目标常常是 stabilizing patient 和识别危险。
常见误区 2：有 protocol 就没有不确定性。实际上 protocol 仍然需要判断。
常见误区 3：临床推理只是复杂理论。实际上它很大一部分是有纪律的优先级排序。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: diagnosis is always the first goal. In reality, the first goal is often stabilizing the patient and identifying danger.
Common confusion 2: protocols eliminate uncertainty. In reality, protocols still require judgment.
Common confusion 3: clinical reasoning is complex theory. In reality, much of it is disciplined prioritization.

reviews.summaryZh: 沟通与记录，让 patient care 不会在一个 provider 说完话、或一支团队离开现场时就断掉。这一章教学生如何清楚传递 clinical meaning、保持连续照护、支持后续 care，并形成足够准确的记录，好让它既能指导 medicine，也能支持 quality review 和法律责任。真正目标是让 care 可转移、可接续，而不是只是“做过了”。
reviews.summaryEn: Communication and documentation ensure that patient care does not stop when one provider is finished speaking or one team leaves the scene. This chapter teaches how to transfer clinical meaning clearly, preserve continuity, support downstream care, and create records that are accurate enough to guide medicine, quality review, and legal accountability. The goal is to make care transferable, not merely performed.
highlightsZh: 只有接手团队真正理解重点，沟通才算成功。
记录是病人照护的一部分，不只是法律防御。
交班时信息顺序和清晰度都很重要。
无线电沟通会影响接收准备和资源使用。
即使治疗做对了，差的沟通也可能造成伤害。
highlightsEn: Communication is successful only when the next team truly understands what matters.
Documentation is part of patient care, not just legal protection.
The order and clarity of handoff matter.
Radio communication affects preparation and resource use.
Poor communication can create harm even when the treatment itself was correct.
keyPointsZh: handoff 只有在下一支团队真的听懂了什么最重要、病情发生了什么变化、做过什么、还剩下什么威胁时，才算成功。
好的沟通是有结构的沟通。信息顺序很重要，因为紧急信息必须先被听到，而不是淹没在非紧急细节里。
documentation 不是把数据全倒进去，而是把 findings、interventions、reasoning、时间中的变化，以及能支撑 handoff 的信息，形成一份有临床意义的记录。
无线电沟通会直接影响资源准备和接收团队 readiness，所以它不是到院后才起作用，而是在到院前就已经影响 care。
即使治疗本身技术上做对了，差的沟通仍然会伤病人，因为不能被理解的 care，就无法被安全接下去。
这一章真正训练学生理解：沟通不是和 clinical quality 分开的，而是 clinical quality 形成机制的一部分。
keyPointsEn: A handoff is only successful if the next team actually understands what matters, what changed, what was done, and what still threatens the patient.
Good communication is organized communication. Information order matters because urgent information must be heard before non-urgent detail.
Documentation is not a data dump. It is a clinically meaningful record of findings, interventions, reasoning, change over time, and handoff-ready information.
Radio communication influences resource preparation and receiving-team readiness. It therefore affects care before arrival, not just after arrival.
Poor communication can damage patients even when treatment itself was technically correct, because care that cannot be understood cannot be continued safely.
This chapter teaches that communication is not separate from clinical quality; it is one of the mechanisms that creates clinical quality.
mustKnowZh: care 必须可转移、可接续。
信息顺序很重要。
documentation 同时服务 medicine、quality 和 legal accountability。
差的沟通本身就是 patient-safety 问题。
mustKnowEn: Care must be transferable.
Information order matters.
Documentation supports medicine, quality, and legal accountability together.
Poor communication is a patient-safety problem.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：只要我都讲了，沟通就算成功。实际上关键在于对方听懂了什么、记住了什么。
常见误区 2：documentation 主要是为了法律。实际上它同时也是连续照护和质量改进工具。
常见误区 3：交班只是形式。实际上它常常决定下一阶段照护能不能正确开始。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: if I said everything, communication was successful. In reality, what matters is what the other team understood and retained.
Common confusion 2: documentation exists mainly for legal reasons. In reality, it is also a continuity and quality tool.
Common confusion 3: handoff is just a formality. In reality, it may determine whether the next phase of care starts correctly.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury
reviews.summaryZh: 创伤系统与受伤机制，是 trauma care 最前端的推理引擎。这一章不只是让你描述发生了什么，而是训练学生用受伤的物理过程去预测隐藏损伤，在身体还没完全把问题表现出来前就先估算严重度，并选择最匹配病人真实风险的紧迫度与目的地。更深层的 lesson 是：trauma thinking 在 full physical exam 结束前就已经开始了。
reviews.summaryEn: Trauma systems and mechanism of injury are the reasoning engine at the front of trauma care. This chapter is not only about describing what happened. It teaches students how to use the physics of injury to predict hidden damage, estimate severity before the body fully declares it, and choose the destination and urgency that best match the patient’s real risk. The deeper lesson is that trauma thinking begins before the full physical exam is finished.
highlightsZh: 受伤机制是预测工具，不是装饰性背景。
能量传递往往在详细查体前就已经透露风险。
创伤目的地很重要，因为 definitive care 能力真的会改变结局。
看起来还稳定的 trauma 病人，也可能依然高风险。
trauma reasoning 在 head-to-toe exam 完成前就已经开始了。
highlightsEn: Mechanism of injury is predictive, not decorative background.
Energy transfer often reveals risk before anatomy is fully examined.
Trauma destination matters because definitive care capability matters.
A stable-looking trauma patient may still be high risk.
Trauma thinking begins before the head-to-toe exam is finished.
keyPointsZh: 受伤机制之所以值钱，是因为力量会先留下线索，而解剖损伤不一定马上全露出来。病人外观看起来还行时，机制本身就可能已经提示严重内伤。
能量传递是核心。力量有多大、从哪个方向来、集中在哪、通过什么路径进入身体，都会帮助你预测哪些结构最可能受压或受损。
一个好的 trauma provider 不只问“眼前看到了什么伤”，还会问“还有什么没看到但很可能已经发生”。机制的价值，就在于把它变成早期危险识别。
创伤系统重要，是因为 definitive care 的能力重要。目的地决策不是单纯 transport decision，而是“真正解决问题会在哪里发生”的 treatment decision。
机制应该影响紧迫度、怀疑程度和 exam 深度。它不能替代 exam，但绝对会塑造 exam。
这一章真正训练的是 trauma 的 pretest thinking：在身体还没把答案全部说出来之前，机制本身就已经在告诉你该往哪里看、该担心到什么程度。
keyPointsEn: Mechanism of injury is valuable because force leaves clues before anatomy becomes obvious. A patient may look relatively intact while the mechanism already suggests major internal injury.
Energy transfer is central. The amount, direction, concentration, and route of force all help predict what structures were likely stressed or damaged.
A good trauma provider asks not just what injury is visible, but what unseen injury is probable. This is how mechanism turns into early danger recognition.
Trauma systems matter because definitive care capability matters. Destination decisions are not simply transport decisions; they are treatment decisions about where real problem-solving can happen.
Mechanism should influence urgency, suspicion, and exam depth. It should not replace the exam, but it should absolutely shape it.
This chapter teaches pretest thinking in trauma: before the body fully reveals the answer, the mechanism may already be telling you where to look and how worried to be.
mustKnowZh: 机制能预测隐藏损伤。
能量传递帮助你及早估算严重度。
目的地选择属于 trauma treatment 的一部分。
trauma 推理在完整 exam 前就已经开始。
mustKnowEn: Mechanism predicts hidden injury.
Energy transfer helps estimate severity early.
Destination choice is part of trauma treatment.
Trauma reasoning starts before the whole exam is complete.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：机制只是背景故事。实际上它常常在查体前就先告诉你危险在哪里。
常见误区 2：能走能说的 trauma 病人就一定低风险。实际上代偿常会暂时掩盖严重内伤。
常见误区 3：最近医院一定是正确目的地。实际上系统能力更重要。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: mechanism is just extra story. In reality, it often predicts danger before the exam does.
Common confusion 2: a walking or talking trauma patient must be low risk. In reality, compensation may temporarily hide serious injury.
Common confusion 3: the nearest hospital is always the right destination. In reality, system capability matters.

reviews.summaryZh: 出血与休克这一章，讲的是 EMS 最时间敏感的真相之一：病人失去组织灌注能力，往往发生在“看起来很夸张地崩掉”之前。这一章不只是让你看见血，而是让你把 hemorrhage 理解成 physiology 问题，学会在代偿还没破掉前就认出危险，并明白 shock 的危险不只是血压数字，而是持续进展的氧输送失败。
reviews.summaryEn: Bleeding and shock teach one of the most time-sensitive truths in EMS: a patient can lose the ability to perfuse tissues long before collapse becomes visually dramatic. This chapter is not just about seeing blood. It is about understanding hemorrhage as a physiology problem, recognizing compensation before it fails, and realizing that the danger of shock is not the blood pressure number alone but the progressive inability to deliver oxygen to vital tissue.
highlightsZh: 休克在变成血压问题前，首先是灌注问题。
可见出血只是危险的一部分。
代偿性休克很会骗人。
骨盆、腹部和胸腔出血，即使没有夸张体表表现，也可能非常严重。
越早识别，后面的处理和目的地选择就越容易改写结局。
highlightsEn: Shock is a perfusion problem before it becomes a blood-pressure problem.
Visible bleeding is only part of the danger.
Compensated shock is deceptive.
Pelvic, abdominal, and thoracic bleeding may be major even without dramatic external signs.
Early recognition changes everything from treatment to destination choice.
keyPointsZh: hemorrhage 会变得致命，是因为它最终造成的是组织缺氧和灌注失败。血液之所以关键，是因为它承担氧输送并维持循环。
代偿性休克危险，是因为病人外表可能还暂时撑得住，但底层 physiology 已经在失败。所以微妙 signs 才这么重要。
皮肤表现、意识状态、脉搏质量、焦虑、毛细血管再充盈和整体外观，很多时候会比血压更早暴露 perfusion problem。
外出血只是问题的一部分。胸腔、腹腔、骨盆和长骨都可能隐藏大量失血，从而改写整张 clinical picture。
EMS 越早识别 hemorrhage physiology，越能把止血、转运紧迫度和目的地选择更好地对齐到生存目标上。
这一章真正训练学生的是：按“循环如何随时间失败”去思考，而不是只问“现在有没有看到血”。
keyPointsEn: Hemorrhage becomes deadly when it causes tissue hypoxia and perfusion failure. The blood itself matters because it carries oxygen and sustains circulation.
Compensated shock is dangerous because the patient may still look temporarily maintainable while physiology is already failing underneath. This is why subtle signs matter.
Skin signs, mentation, pulse quality, anxiety, capillary refill, and overall appearance often reveal perfusion problems before blood pressure does.
External bleeding is only part of the problem. Chest, abdomen, pelvis, and long bones may all hide major blood loss that changes the whole clinical picture.
The earlier EMS recognizes hemorrhage physiology, the more effectively hemorrhage control, transport urgency, and destination choice can be aligned with survival.
This chapter teaches the student to think in terms of circulation failure over time, not just whether blood is visible right now.
mustKnowZh: 休克首先是 perfusion problem。
代偿会掩盖严重度。
可见出血不是 hemorrhage 的全部故事。
越早识别，越能改变 outcome。
mustKnowEn: Shock is a perfusion problem first.
Compensation can hide severity.
Visible bleeding is not the whole hemorrhage story.
Early recognition changes outcome.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：血压还行，就没有休克。实际上病人可能还在代偿。
常见误区 2：外面看不到大出血，就说明问题不大。实际上隐匿性出血可能非常致命。
常见误区 3：休克识别主要靠 monitor。实际上很多时候病人先通过外观和灌注线索告诉你。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: if blood pressure is okay, there is no shock. In reality, compensation may still be active.
Common confusion 2: if the bleeding is not obvious externally, it is probably minor. In reality, hidden bleeding can be catastrophic.
Common confusion 3: shock recognition belongs to the monitor. In reality, the patient often tells you first through appearance and perfusion clues.

reviews.summaryZh: 软组织损伤看起来常像“只是伤口”，但它背后可能隐藏大量失血、深层组织损伤、污染、感染风险、挤压伤生理变化，以及爆炸伤带来的复杂机制。这一章的重点，是让学生不再把 wound 当成单纯皮肤问题，而是把 soft-tissue injury 当成与机制、出血、组织保护和全身风险都相关的伤。真正目标是准确判断严重度、先控制当前威胁，再避免现场处理本身把组织伤得更重。
reviews.summaryEn: Soft-tissue injuries may look simple on the surface, but they can hide major bleeding, deep structural damage, contamination, infection risk, crush physiology, and blast-related complexity. This chapter teaches students to stop thinking of wounds as skin problems and start thinking about tissue injury as a whole-body and mechanism-linked problem. The goal is to recognize severity accurately, control immediate threats, and protect injured tissue from becoming more damaged during field care.
highlightsZh: 软组织伤不只是皮肤问题。
体表外观常会低估内部严重度。
止血和组织保护是核心优先级。
挤压伤和爆炸伤往往隐藏复杂损伤。
伤口处理不是把它盖住而已，而是生理与损伤控制问题。
highlightsEn: Soft-tissue injury is more than a skin problem.
External appearance may underestimate internal severity.
Bleeding control and tissue protection are core priorities.
Crush and blast injuries often involve hidden complexity.
Wound care is about physiology and damage control, not just covering the wound.
keyPointsZh: 开放性和闭合性软组织损伤都可能很危险。开放伤看起来明显，但闭合伤同样可能隐藏严重出血、肿胀、间隔室风险或深层组织破坏。
伤口评估不能只看表面，要同时看出血程度、组织暴露、污染、疼痛、功能、机制，以及是否合并血管、神经或肌骨损伤。
止血常常是第一优先级，因为一旦出血持续，问题就会从局部组织伤快速变成全身性休克问题。压力、敷料、适当时的 wound packing 和持续 reassessment 都属于真正有意义的 wound care。
挤压伤危险，是因为外面看起来可能没那么夸张，但里面的肌肉破坏、肿胀、血管受压和后续全身并发问题可能非常重。provider 必须学会超越外观去想。
爆炸伤很少只是一个伤口。它往往同时包括压力波伤、碎片穿透伤、烧伤、抛掷伤和隐藏内伤，所以机制本身就该让你想到多系统损伤。
软组织处理的目的不是为了把 wound 弄得好看，而是为了止血、保护暴露组织、降低感染风险、减少搬运中的进一步损伤，并在转运途中持续保护组织。
keyPointsEn: Open and closed soft-tissue injuries can both be dangerous. A dramatic open wound may be obvious, but a closed soft-tissue injury can still hide severe bleeding, swelling, compartment concerns, or deep tissue damage.
Wound assessment should include bleeding severity, tissue exposure, contamination, pain, function, mechanism, and the possibility of associated vascular, neurologic, or musculoskeletal injury.

Bleeding control is often the first priority because blood loss can rapidly shift the problem from local tissue injury to systemic shock. Pressure, dressings, packing when appropriate, and constant reassessment are all part of meaningful wound care.

Crush injuries are dangerous because the visible external injury may underestimate internal muscle destruction, swelling, vascular compromise, and later systemic complications. The field provider must think beyond appearance.

Blast injuries are rarely just a wound. They may involve pressure effects, penetrating fragments, burns, displacement injury, and hidden internal trauma. The mechanism itself raises suspicion for multi-system injury.

The purpose of soft-tissue management is not cosmetic cleanup. It is hemorrhage control, protection of exposed or damaged tissue, infection-risk reduction, pain-aware handling, and prevention of further injury during transport.
mustKnowZh: 绝不要只凭皮肤外观判断软组织伤的严重度。
很多时候止血比 wound 细节更优先。
挤压伤和爆炸伤必须按机制去思考。
任何 wound care 后都要 reassess。
mustKnowEn: Never judge soft-tissue severity by skin appearance alone.
Bleeding control often matters before wound detail.
Crush and blast injuries require mechanism-based thinking.
Wound care should always include reassessment.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：伤口看起来小，损伤就一定小。实际上深度和机制可能让它非常严重。
常见误区 2：wound care 主要就是包扎。实际上核心是止血、组织保护和风险识别。
常见误区 3：闭合性软组织伤一定比开放伤风险低。实际上隐藏出血和肿胀可能同样严重。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: if the wound looks small, the injury must be small. In reality, depth and mechanism may make it severe.
Common confusion 2: wound care is mostly about bandaging. In reality, it is about hemorrhage control, tissue protection, and risk recognition.
Common confusion 3: closed soft-tissue injury is automatically lower risk than open injury. In reality, hidden bleeding and swelling can be serious.

reviews.summaryZh: 烧伤不是单纯的皮肤受伤，它是热、化学、电或辐射造成的损伤，可能同时打乱液体平衡、灌注、疼痛控制、感染风险、气道状态和整体生理。这一章训练学生超越看见的烧伤面积，去理解深度、范围、部位、机制、吸入伤风险和全身后果。真正目标是分清：谁只是局部损伤，谁已经开始全身不稳，谁需要 burn center 等更高级资源。
reviews.summaryEn: Burns are not simply skin injuries—they are thermal, chemical, electrical, or radiation-related injuries that may disrupt fluid balance, perfusion, pain control, infection risk, airway status, and overall physiology. This chapter teaches students to move beyond the visible burn and think in terms of depth, extent, location, mechanism, inhalation risk, and systemic consequence. The goal is to recognize who can be managed simply, who is physiologically unstable, and who requires specialized burn-focused care.
highlightsZh: 烧伤是全身性问题，不只是皮肤问题。
深度、范围、部位和吸入伤风险都重要。
气道受累有时比肉眼可见烧伤更危险。
严重烧伤必须加入液体与灌注思维。
烧伤严重度靠生理判断，不只靠外观。
highlightsEn: Burns are systemic problems, not just skin problems.
Depth, extent, location, and inhalation risk all matter.
Airway involvement may be more dangerous than the visible burn.
Fluid and perfusion thinking are essential in serious burns.
Burn severity is judged by physiology, not appearance alone.
keyPointsZh: 烧伤评估首先从机制开始。热烧伤、化学烧伤、电烧伤和辐射烧伤的行为并不一样，每一种都会造成不同的局部和全身损伤模式。
深度重要，因为浅表、部分层和全层烧伤在组织后果和愈合意义上完全不同。范围也重要，因为烧伤越大，就越可能引发全身不稳。
部位非常重要。面部、气道、手、足、会阴和大关节附近的烧伤，常会带来单看面积看不出来的严重并发问题。
对烧伤病人的 airway assessment 非常关键，尤其当存在吸入伤怀疑、面部烧伤、密闭空间暴露、烟灰、声音嘶哑或进行性肿胀时。外观看起来不算很大的烧伤，依然可能变成气道急症。
大面积或重度烧伤会造成明显液体转移、灌注问题、热量丢失、剧痛和感染脆弱性。所以严重烧伤必须按 whole-patient thinking 处理，而不是只把它当 wound。
EMS 早期 burn care 的目标，是阻止持续损伤、保护病人、减少热量丢失与污染、监测气道和休克问题，并识别谁需要 specialized transport 或更高级资源。
keyPointsEn: Burn assessment starts with mechanism. Thermal, chemical, electrical, and radiation burns do not all behave the same way, and each mechanism creates different patterns of local and systemic injury.

Depth matters because superficial, partial-thickness, and full-thickness injuries do not carry the same tissue consequences or healing implications. Extent matters because larger burns are more likely to cause systemic instability.
Location matters greatly. Burns involving the face, airway, hands, feet, genitalia, or major joints may create complications that are not fully captured by total body surface area alone.

Airway assessment is critical in burn patients, especially when there is suspicion of inhalation injury, facial burns, enclosed-space exposure, soot, hoarseness, or progressive swelling. A visually modest burn can still become an airway emergency.

Large or severe burns can cause major fluid shifts, perfusion problems, heat loss, pain, and infection vulnerability. Serious burn care therefore requires whole-patient thinking, not wound-only thinking.

The purpose of early burn care in EMS is to stop ongoing injury, protect the patient, limit heat loss and contamination, monitor for airway and shock problems, and identify who needs specialized transport or higher-level resources.
mustKnowZh: 不要只凭外观看烧伤严重度。
气道风险有时比可见烧伤更重要。
大面积或深烧伤属于全身性急症。
很多时候部位和面积同样重要。
mustKnowEn: Do not judge burn severity by surface appearance alone.
Airway risk can outweigh visible burn severity.
Large or deep burns are systemic emergencies.
Location matters as much as size in many cases.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：烧伤严重度就是看皮肤看起来多惨。实际上生理状态和气道风险可能更重要。
常见误区 2：只要算面积就够了。实际上深度和部位同样关键。
常见误区 3：看起来局部，问题就一定局部。实际上大烧伤是 whole-patient problem。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: burn severity equals what the skin looks like. In reality, physiology and airway risk may matter more.
Common confusion 2: total body surface area alone determines seriousness. In reality, depth and location are also critical.
Common confusion 3: if the burn looks local, the problem is local. In reality, major burns are whole-patient problems.

reviews.summaryZh: 头面部损伤绝不是外观问题而已。这类损伤可能同时牵涉大脑、气道、出血、眼睛、口腔、前颈部以及多种神经功能。这一章训练学生超越看起来很吓人的表面，去思考 airway compromise、创伤性脑损伤、感觉器官受损、肿胀，以及正在变化的 neurologic status。真正目标是：不要被外观带偏，却又能及时抓住最先致命的风险。
reviews.summaryEn: Head and face injuries demand more than cosmetic attention. These injuries may involve the brain, airway, bleeding, the eyes, the mouth, the anterior neck, and multiple neurologic functions at the same time. This chapter teaches students how to see beyond the visible injury and think about airway compromise, traumatic brain injury, sensory loss, swelling, and evolving neurologic change. The goal is to avoid being distracted by dramatic appearance while still recognizing what may become life-threatening first.
highlightsZh: 头面部损伤可能同时威胁气道、脑功能和循环。
外观看起来很吓人，但优先级仍然要回到 airway、neurologic status 和 perfusion。
面部出血和肿胀可能很快制造气道问题。
神经变化比看起来多惨更重要。
眼、口、耳和前颈部损伤都会提供重要临床信息。
highlightsEn: Head and face injury can threaten airway, brain function, and circulation at the same time.
Appearance may be dramatic, but priority still belongs to airway, neurologic status, and perfusion.
Facial bleeding and swelling can rapidly create airway problems.
Neurologic change matters more than external drama.
Eye, mouth, ear, and anterior neck injuries all add important clinical information.
keyPointsZh: 头部损伤评估绝不只是看表面伤口。更大的问题是：脑有没有受伤、神经功能有没有变化、病人是不是越来越失去保护 airway 的能力。
面部损伤特别危险，是因为它可能大量出血、迅速肿胀、扭曲正常解剖，并影响气道通畅。所以 face injury 很容易从局部伤变成 airway problem。
头部创伤里的 neurologic assessment 要注意意识状态、反应性、必要时的瞳孔发现、运动、语言，以及时间中的变化。趋势很重要，因为恶化不一定会立刻出现。
眼、耳、口腔和前颈部不是附属细节，它们常常能揭示受伤机制、严重度、功能损失、气道风险，以及会改变处理策略的相关损伤。
这一章训练的是有纪律的优先级思维：不要被血多、脸部很惨这些外观拖走注意力，而忽略真正先致命的东西——airway、ventilation、perfusion 和 brain function。
